title: The Hidden Cost of Growth: AI Data Centers and the Global Water Challenge
subtitle: As cities struggle with extreme heat, tech companies face new questions about how much water they need to keep their systems running.

summary.en: Amazon has released its first report on the water usage of its global data centers. The company used 2.5 billion gallons of water in 2025 to keep its computer servers cool. This report comes as cities around the world are facing record-breaking heat waves. Experts warn that rising temperatures are putting pressure on both public health and local water supplies. Tech companies now face increasing pressure to balance the growth of artificial intelligence with the need for environmental sustainability.
summary.zh: 亞馬遜發布了首份關於其全球資料中心用水量的報告。該公司在 2025 年消耗了 25 億加侖的水來冷卻電腦伺服器。這份報告發布之際，全球各地的城市正遭遇破紀錄的熱浪。專家警告，氣溫升高正對公共衛生與在地供水造成壓力。科技公司目前面臨越來越大的壓力，必須在人工智慧的成長與環境永續的需求之間取得平衡。

paragraphs.en: As the world faces rising temperatures and more frequent heat waves, the rapid growth of artificial intelligence is creating a new challenge for cities. Modern data centers, which power the AI tools used by millions, require massive amounts of electricity and water to stay cool. This issue has become a major topic of debate, as urban areas struggle to manage their own water supplies and energy needs during the hottest months of the year.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著全球面臨氣溫升高與熱浪頻繁來襲，人工智慧的快速發展正為城市帶來新的挑戰。現代資料中心為數百萬人使用的人工智慧工具提供動力，但這些設施需要消耗大量的電力與水資源來進行冷卻。此議題已成為主要的辯論焦點，因為都會區在一年中最炎熱的月份，正面臨難以兼顧居民用水與能源需求的困境。

paragraphs.en: In a recent development, Amazon has shared its annual water usage data for the first time. According to The Verge, the company’s global data center operations consumed 2.5 billion gallons of water in 2025. This report was released shortly after officials in Seattle decided to pause the construction of new data centers for one year, a move that some of Amazon’s own employees had supported. The company noted that its water usage actually dropped by two percent compared to 2024, even as its operations continued to expand.
paragraphs.zh: 近期，亞馬遜 (Amazon) 首次公開了其年度用水數據。根據科技媒體「邊緣」(The Verge) 報導，該公司 2025 年在全球的資料中心營運共消耗了 25 億加侖的水。這份報告發布的時間點，正值西雅圖 (Seattle) 官員決定暫停新建資料中心一年，而這項決策也獲得了部分亞馬遜員工的支持。該公司指出，儘管營運規模持續擴張，但其用水量相較於 2024 年其實下降了百分之二。

paragraphs.en: Amazon claims that its data centers are highly efficient. The company stated that about 90 percent of the time, its facilities use air cooling rather than water. It only uses evaporative water cooling—a process that uses water to lower the temperature of hot air—during the hottest hours of the year. Based on an adjusted number from a research paper, Amazon claims its centers are seven times more water-efficient than the industry average. However, the report from The Verge points out that Amazon’s data does not include the water used to generate the electricity that powers its servers, nor does it count the water used during the construction of new buildings.
paragraphs.zh: 亞馬遜聲稱其資料中心具有極高的效率。該公司表示，約有百分之九十的時間，其設施是採用空氣冷卻而非水冷卻。僅在一年中最炎熱的時段，才會使用蒸發式水冷卻系統，這是一種利用水來降低熱空氣溫度的程序。根據一份研究報告調整後的數據，亞馬遜宣稱其中心的用水效率比產業平均水準高出七倍。然而，「邊緣」的報導指出，亞馬遜的數據並未包含發電給伺服器使用所消耗的水量，也沒有計入興建新建築時的用水。

paragraphs.en: While tech companies work to improve their efficiency, cities are facing a different kind of pressure. According to Deutsche Welle, urban areas are becoming "heat islands," where paved roads and a lack of green space make cities significantly hotter than surrounding rural areas. This extra heat is not just uncomfortable; it is a serious public health risk. The World Health Organization has described heat as a "silent killer," noting that nearly half a million people die every year from heat-related causes. As climate change makes heat waves longer and more intense, cities are finding it harder to keep their residents safe.
paragraphs.zh: 當科技公司致力於提升效率的同時，城市正面臨另一種壓力。根據德國之聲 (Deutsche Welle) 報導，都會區正逐漸成為「熱島」，鋪設的道路與缺乏綠地使得城市比周邊鄉村地區更加炎熱。這種額外的熱能不僅讓人感到不適，更構成嚴重的公共衛生風險。世界衛生組織 (World Health Organization) 將高溫形容為「沉默的殺手」，並指出每年有近五十萬人死於與高溫相關的原因。隨著氣候變遷導致熱浪持續時間更長、強度更高，城市要保障居民安全變得愈發困難。

paragraphs.en: In cities like Teresina, Brazil, temperatures now frequently go above 40 degrees Celsius. This disrupts daily life, affecting everything from sleep quality to the productivity of the local population. Similarly, in Antalya, Turkey, officials are preparing for the upcoming COP31 climate talks while dealing with summer heat that is becoming more frequent and severe. Melike Kireccibasi, a climate expert in Antalya, told Deutsche Welle that the changing nature of the heat is placing "increasing pressure" on the city’s health services, energy systems, and water supplies.
paragraphs.zh: 在巴西的特雷西納 (Teresina) 等城市，氣溫經常飆破攝氏 40 度。這擾亂了日常生活，從睡眠品質到當地民眾的生產力都受到影響。同樣地，在土耳其的安塔利亞 (Antalya)，官員們正一邊籌備即將到來的第 31 屆聯合國氣候變遷大會 (COP31)，一邊應對日益頻繁且嚴峻的夏季高溫。安塔利亞的氣候專家基雷奇巴希 (Melike Kireccibasi) 向德國之聲表示，氣候變遷導致的高溫型態正對該市的醫療服務、能源系統與供水造成「日益沉重的壓力」。

paragraphs.en: There is a growing tension between the needs of the tech industry and the needs of local communities. While Amazon argues that it is more efficient than rivals like Google, Microsoft, and Meta, the comparison is complicated. For example, reports suggest that some data from rivals may focus specifically on centers used for AI, while Amazon’s report covers all of its operations. This makes it difficult for the public to know exactly how much water the AI industry as a whole is consuming.
paragraphs.zh: 科技產業的需求與在地社區的需求之間，緊張關係正逐漸升溫。雖然亞馬遜主張其效率優於 Google、微軟 (Microsoft) 與 Meta 等競爭對手，但這種比較相當複雜。例如，有報導指出，部分競爭對手的數據可能專注於人工智慧專用的中心，而亞馬遜的報告則涵蓋了所有營運項目。這使得大眾難以確切得知人工智慧產業整體究竟消耗了多少水資源。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the challenge for cities will be to adapt to a warming world while managing the demands of new technology. Experts suggest that better building design, such as using materials that reflect heat or adding green roofs, can help reduce the need for constant air conditioning. However, as the demand for AI continues to rise, the debate over how much water these massive computing facilities should be allowed to use is likely to continue. With cities already struggling to provide enough water for their residents during extreme heat, the question of how to balance technological progress with environmental safety remains a critical issue for the future.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，城市面臨的挑戰將是在管理新科技需求的同時，適應暖化的世界。專家建議，透過更佳的建築設計，例如使用反射熱能的建材或增設綠屋頂，有助於減少對持續開冷氣的依賴。然而，隨著對人工智慧的需求持續攀升，關於這些大型運算設施應被允許使用多少水資源的辯論，預計將持續下去。在城市已難以在極端高溫下為居民提供足夠用水的情況下，如何平衡科技進步與環境安全，仍是未來至關重要的議題。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop an activity or process for a short period of time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（暫停），這裡作動詞用。文中：This report was released shortly after officials in Seattle decided to pause the construction of new data centers for one year, a move that some of Amazon’s own employees had supported.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To draw attention to a particular fact or piece of information.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞 point 和介系詞 out 組成，意思與單字原意不同。文中：However, the report from The Verge points out that Amazon’s data does not include the water used to generate the electricity that powers its servers, nor does it count the water used during the construction of new buildings.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To interrupt an event, activity, or process by causing a disturbance or problem.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此字在新聞英語中常形容對生活或系統的干擾。文中：This disrupts daily life, affecting everything from sleep quality to the productivity of the local population.

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Amazon’s data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water last year

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: As the world heats up, cities work to cool down

previousArticles.title: The Hidden Cost of AI: Balancing Tech Growth and Water Security
previousArticles.summaryEn: Amazon has released its first report on annual water usage for its global data centers. The company consumed 2.5 billion gallons of water in 2025 to keep its servers cool. This comes as cities worldwide struggle with rising temperatures and limited water supplies. Experts warn that the growth of artificial intelligence requires careful resource management. Tech firms are now under pressure to prove they can operate efficiently without harming local communities.

previousArticles.title: Global Banks Increase Fossil Fuel Funding Despite Climate Goals
previousArticles.summaryEn: A recent report reveals that the world's 65 largest banks provided $906 billion to fossil fuel companies last year. This represents an increase of $64 billion compared to the previous year. Experts warn that this funding makes it difficult to meet international climate goals. While some banks have reduced their support, others continue to invest heavily in expanding oil and gas production. The findings highlight a growing tension between banking practices and global efforts to stop rising temperatures.

previousArticles.title: Global Banks Increase Fossil Fuel Funding Despite Climate Goals
previousArticles.summaryEn: A new report shows that the world's largest banks provided $906 billion to fossil fuel companies last year. This increase in funding makes it harder to meet international climate targets. Meanwhile, officials at the upcoming Cop31 summit are calling for a shift toward electricity to reduce carbon emissions. Experts warn that current investments lock the world into a risky and unreliable energy system. The global community faces a difficult choice between continuing with fossil fuels or moving toward a cleaner future.

quiz.question: According to the article, how does Amazon manage cooling in its data centers?

options.A: It uses only water-based cooling systems to ensure maximum efficiency.
options.B: It primarily uses air cooling, reserving water-based cooling for the hottest periods.
options.C: It relies entirely on air cooling to avoid using any water resources.
options.D: It uses evaporative cooling 90 percent of the time to maintain server temperatures.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出，Amazon 的設施約有 90% 的時間使用空氣冷卻，僅在一年中最炎熱的時段才使用蒸發式水冷系統。選項 A 錯誤，因為這與文章所述相反；選項 C 錯誤，因為並非「完全」不使用水；選項 D 錯誤，數據對調了，90% 是指空氣冷卻而非水冷。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the reported water usage of tech companies like Amazon?

options.A: The reported figures provide a complete and accurate picture of total environmental impact.
options.B: Tech companies are deliberately hiding their water usage to avoid public criticism.
options.C: The reported data may underestimate the true water footprint of the industry.
options.D: Amazon is currently the most water-efficient company in the entire tech industry.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段與第六段，Amazon 的報告未包含發電用水及建築施工用水，且與競爭對手的數據比較標準不一，因此可以推論目前報告的數據可能低估了實際的總體耗水量。選項 A 錯誤，因為數據並不完整；選項 B 無法由文中證實；選項 D 錯誤，文中僅提到 Amazon 自稱比平均水平高效，且與對手的比較因標準不同而難以定論。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'heat islands' refer to?

options.A: Isolated rural areas that are naturally cooler than cities.
options.B: Urban locations that experience higher temperatures than their surroundings.
options.C: Coastal cities that are at risk of being flooded by rising sea levels.
options.D: Buildings designed with green roofs to help cool down the city.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段上下文，作者提到鋪設的道路與缺乏綠地使得城市比周邊農村地區更熱，因此「heat islands」（熱島）指的是這種城市比周邊環境更熱的現象。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 與熱島效應的定義無關；選項 D 是解決方案，而非熱島效應本身。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: Amazon is leading the way in making data centers more environmentally friendly.
options.B: Cities must stop the construction of all new data centers to save water.
options.C: The rapid expansion of AI is creating a conflict between technological progress and urban resource management.
options.D: Climate change is primarily caused by the excessive water consumption of large tech companies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章從頭到尾都在探討 AI 科技發展對城市水資源與能源的壓力，並強調城市在面對極端氣候時，必須在科技需求與居民生活安全之間取得平衡。選項 A 僅是文章的一個細節；選項 B 過於極端，文中並未主張全面禁止；選項 D 將氣候變遷的原因過度簡化，並非文章主旨。
